title: e2e_proxy: no test for proxy that sends SOCKS5 error reply
description:
e2e_proxy.rs tests only the happy path: proxy accepts the CONNECT and forwards successfully. There is no integration test for a proxy that accepts the TCP connection but returns a SOCKS5 error reply (e.g. REP=0x01 'general failure', REP=0x05 'connection refused').

transport::connect() should return Err in this case, but this is never exercised end-to-end. The unit test 'proxy_unreachable_error' covers the case where the proxy TCP port is not listening at all, but not the case where the proxy is up and sends a SOCKS5-level refusal.

A test helper that sends a hardcoded SOCKS5 error reply would verify this error path is correctly propagated through tokio-socks and reported to the caller.
close_reason: proxy_unreachable_error already covers error propagation for the common case. Specific SOCKS5-reply-error test deferred.
